content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates all four VIEW components consistently. Impartiality is striking: when the client says 'we got to hold off on the insights,' the coach simply says 'Okay' (turn 14) — no defense, no explanation, just receiving the calibration. Vulnerability appears in turn 22's unadorned 'I'm sorry. I'm just sorry that that's the case for you' — not a coaching move, just human contact — and in turn 30's personal disclosure offered as solidarity rather than authority. Wonder is genuine: questions like 'Is there anything about rushing through your day that can't be enjoyed?' and 'How would you enjoy your shame?' point at the actual phenomenon present, not at a hypothetical future state. Empathy is felt rather than performed — no filler validations, no 'thank you for sharing.' The coach holds no agenda for the client to land anywhere: when the client says 'you're a victim, bro,' the coach affirms the very identity the client is resisting, which is only possible from non-attachment. Pace mismatches when needed (the coach slows after turn 13's pushback) and the questions arrive one at a time with space to land.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 8, 14, 16, 22, 26, 30, 34, 36, 40],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Inverts the future-frame into NOW from genuine curiosity, not as a technique-flex."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names what is actually seen — smile vs confused head — from observation, not interpretation. Wonder for the live phenomenon."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Okay.' Receives the client's calibration without defense. Pure impartiality and undefendedness."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reframe emerges from wonder rather than from a fix-it agenda — the rush itself could be the place of enjoyment."},
    {"turn": 18, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Coach checks his own read ('some version of that is what I'm asking') — willing to be wrong."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Vulnerability and empathy as actual contact, not a coaching move. Doesn't try to transform the pain."},
    {"turn": 26, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Affirms victim-status the client is resisting — impartial, undefended, not invested in the client feeling better."},
    {"turn": 30, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Personal disclosure offered as solidarity ('maybe 10 times in my life...'), not as authority."},
    {"turn": 34, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Question points directly at the live shame; wonder for what is here, not for what's underneath."},
    {"turn": 40, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Experimental framing — client gets to find out. Coach not invested in a particular result."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["frame-it-as-an-experiment", "follow-wonder-flag-the-deflection", "treat-yourself-as-actor-of-emotion", "shame-as-disconnection", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "are-you-bracing-or-embracing", "natural-shame-vs-installed-shame"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The turn-14 'Okay' is the strongest single signal of true VIEW state — most coaches would have defended or explained."
}
